title: Stash Internals, Partial Work, and Portable Patches · Mastery Check
summary: A maintainer review compares near-miss operations for stash internals, partial work, and portable patches and requires the smallest safe work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger with topology and content evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect stash commit structure, parents, refs, reflog, staged state, and untracked representation instead of treating a stash as an external bag.
Use pathspec, patch, staged, keep-index, include-untracked, and all controls while previewing secret and ignored-file consequences.
Choose apply, pop, branch, store, create, export, or import from collision risk, portability, and retention needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Gita Stash Object Model · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Case G88 is a quiz in a disposable repository. only reviewed staged work should move while unstaged experiments remain visible. The learner must predict the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths, run git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip, explain policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection, test this changed condition: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes, and preserve this recovery boundary: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash.

Which action demonstrates Gita Stash Object Model without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inspect stash commit structure, parents, refs, reflog, staged state, and untracked representation instead of treating a stash as an external bag.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Gita Wip Recovery Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G95 is a quiz in a disposable repository. only reviewed staged work should move while unstaged experiments remain visible. The learner must predict the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths, run git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip, explain policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection, test this changed condition: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes, and preserve this recovery boundary: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Create named durable refs or encrypted external backups for important work, verify restoration on a changed base, and expire temporary objects deliberately.
